---
title: "Unreal Engine cutscene experiment"
description: "Video cutscene and programmed object movement in Unreal Engine 4.22, with physics simulation and raytracing enabled. Built from royalty-free assets."
date: 2019-06-01
categories: [Personal, Game Engines, Unreal]
image: ../assets/img/placeholder-square.png
---

::: {.eyebrow}
Personal project
:::

<div style="position:relative; padding-bottom:56.25%; height:0; overflow:hidden; border:1px solid #30363d; border-radius:4px; max-width:680px; margin:1rem 0;">
  <iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/kLks7bYS2z8" style="position:absolute; top:0; left:0; width:100%; height:100%;" frameborder="0" allow="picture-in-picture; encrypted-media" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>

## Idea

An experiment in Unreal Engine 4.22: a video cutscene with programmed
object movement, physics simulation, and raytracing enabled. The point
was the scripting and rendering pipeline, not original art — the scene is
assembled entirely from royalty-free assets.

## Implementation

Scripted object movement, physics-driven secondary motion, and raytracing
on the lighting pass. The cutscene is sequenced as a single take.

## Stack

Unreal Engine 4.22, royalty-free asset packs.
